checkpoints.check: Loading states are visible for operations >1 second
checkpoints.notes: No progress indicators during connection attempts, which can take time.

checkpoints.check: Progress indicators shown for multi-step processes
checkpoints.notes: Connecting to bluetooth devices can be a multi-step process that is not visualized.

checkpoints.check: Success/error feedback appears after user actions
checkpoints.notes: Workout completion shows a summary screen, which is good feedback. Status text for connections is present.

checkpoints.check: Current location is clear in navigation
checkpoints.notes: The active screen is changed by adding/removing an 'active' class. This is functional but lacks strong visual indicators in the overall UI structure (e.g., navigation tabs).

heuristics.name: Match between system and real world

checkpoints.check: Labels use user language, not internal jargon
checkpoints.notes: Labels like 'Power (W)', 'Cadence (RPM)' are clear and standard.

checkpoints.check: Icons follow established conventions
checkpoints.notes: The app uses standard button and text elements, no confusing icons noted.

checkpoints.check: Information appears in natural and logical order
checkpoints.notes: The flow (Connect -> Select -> Workout -> Summary) is logical.

checkpoints.check: Metaphors match real-world expectations
checkpoints.notes: The workout diagram metaphor is a common and effective way to visualize workout structure.

checkpoints.check: Undo is available for destructive actions
checkpoints.notes: No clear 'undo' for actions like starting a workout. No 'stop' button during the workout, only a 'Done' button on the summary screen.

checkpoints.check: Cancel/back options are clearly visible
checkpoints.notes: A dedicated 'Back' or 'Cancel' is missing from most screens.

checkpoints.check: Users can exit flows without losing progress
checkpoints.notes: User cannot easily go back from the workout screen to the selection screen without stopping the workout.

checkpoints.check: Confirmation dialogs for irreversible actions
checkpoints.notes: No confirmation before uploading to Strava.

checkpoints.check: UI elements behave the same way throughout
checkpoints.notes: Buttons and status text appear consistent across screens.

checkpoints.check: Terminology is consistent across all pages
checkpoints.notes: Terminology seems consistent.

checkpoints.check: Visual patterns (spacing, colors) are consistent
checkpoints.notes: The use of CSS variables suggests a consistent approach to styling, which is good.

checkpoints.check: Platform conventions are followed
checkpoints.notes: Follows standard web conventions for a single-page application.

checkpoints.check: Form validation occurs before submission
checkpoints.notes: No complex forms, so this is less of an issue. The agent API for workout creation would be the place for validation.

checkpoints.check: Constraints prevent invalid inputs
checkpoints.notes: The UI is primarily selection-based, which prevents invalid input.

checkpoints.check: Destructive actions require confirmation
checkpoints.notes: Uploading to Strava is a key action that should have a confirmation step.

checkpoints.check: Default values reduce user effort and errors
checkpoints.notes: N/A for the current UI.

checkpoints.check: Options are visible rather than requiring memorization
checkpoints.notes: Workout list is clearly visible.

checkpoints.check: Help and instructions are easily accessible
checkpoints.notes: No visible help or instructions. For example, what are the requirements for connecting a trainer?

checkpoints.check: Recently used items are easily accessible
checkpoints.notes: No concept of 'recently done' workouts to quickly repeat one.

checkpoints.check: Search and filter options are visible
checkpoints.notes: No search or filter for the workout library, which will become an issue as it grows.

checkpoints.check: Keyboard shortcuts available for frequent actions
checkpoints.notes: No keyboard shortcuts. This is a minor issue for this type of app.

checkpoints.check: Customizable interface elements
checkpoints.notes: No customization options.

checkpoints.check: Shortcuts or recent items for repeat tasks
checkpoints.notes: Repeating a workout requires finding it in the list again.

checkpoints.check: Batch operations for power users
checkpoints.notes: No batch operations applicable yet.

checkpoints.check: Each screen focuses on one primary action
checkpoints.notes: Each screen has a clear purpose (Connect, Select, etc.).

checkpoints.check: Visual hierarchy guides attention to important elements
checkpoints.notes: The use of larger font for metric values creates a clear hierarchy.

checkpoints.check: Whitespace used effectively
checkpoints.notes: The layout is clean and not cluttered.

checkpoints.check: No unnecessary decorative elements that distract
checkpoints.notes: The design appears functional and not overly decorative.

heuristics.name: Help users recognize, diagnose, and recover from errors

checkpoints.check: Error messages are in plain language (no codes)
checkpoints.notes: Bluetooth connection can fail for many reasons. The app only shows 'Not connected' or 'Connection failed'.

checkpoints.check: Error messages indicate what went wrong specifically
checkpoints.notes: Error messages are generic.

checkpoints.check: Error messages suggest how to fix the issue
checkpoints.notes: No suggestions on how to resolve a connection failure.

checkpoints.check: Errors are visually prominent and close to the source
checkpoints.notes: Status text is clearly visible.

checkpoints.check: Help is easily accessible from any screen
checkpoints.notes: No help section or documentation is visible.

checkpoints.check: Contextual help is provided where needed
checkpoints.notes: No contextual help like tooltips.

checkpoints.check: Onboarding guides new users through key features
checkpoints.notes: No onboarding for first-time users.

checkpoints.check: Documentation is searchable and task-focused
checkpoints.notes: No documentation.

checkpoints.check: Text meets 4.5:1 contrast ratio (WCAG AA)
checkpoints.notes: Cannot verify all text contrast without viewing, but will assume some may fail. The use of var(--accent-color) for target power needs checking against the background.

checkpoints.check: Large text meets 3:1 contrast ratio
checkpoints.notes: Headings appear large enough.

checkpoints.check: Color is not the only way to convey information
checkpoints.notes: The workout diagram uses color to represent power zones. This should be paired with text or patterns for accessibility.

checkpoints.check: Focus indicators have sufficient contrast
checkpoints.notes: The default browser focus indicator may not have sufficient contrast on all backgrounds.

checkpoints.check: All interactive elements reachable via Tab key
checkpoints.notes: All interactive elements are buttons, which are tabbable by default.

checkpoints.check: Focus order follows logical reading order
checkpoints.notes: DOM order appears logical.

checkpoints.check: Focus ring is visible on all interactive elements
checkpoints.notes: No custom focus styles are defined in style.css, so it relies on the browser default which can sometimes be unclear.

checkpoints.check: No keyboard traps (user can always navigate away)
checkpoints.notes: The app structure is simple, keyboard traps are unlikely.

checkpoints.check: All images have meaningful alt text
checkpoints.notes: No images in the app.

checkpoints.check: Form inputs have associated labels
checkpoints.notes: Buttons have clear text, no separate form inputs yet.

checkpoints.check: ARIA attributes used correctly for dynamic content
checkpoints.notes: Live metric regions (like power, cadence) are not marked with ARIA attributes (e.g., aria-live) to announce updates to screen readers.

checkpoints.check: Headings follow logical hierarchy (h1 > h2 > h3)
checkpoints.notes: The use of h1/h2 seems appropriate.
